config.name: 10_1038_nmeth_3810
config.description: Reproducible pipeline generated from paper (10.1038/nmeth.3810). Assays: eCLIP-seq library preparation, Normalization of eCLIP signal against SMInput, IDR Analysis, Public CLIP Database and iCLIP data processing, Blocking RBP binding by antisense oligonucleotide, RBFOX2-knockdown transcriptome profiling by microarray, eCLIP-seq data processing.

steps.name: eCLIP-seq library preparation — RNA-binding protein–RNA interactions are stabilized by UV cr
steps.description: RNA-binding protein–RNA interactions are stabilized by UV crosslinking of live cells at 254 nm.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --UV_wavelength 254 nm --UV_dose 400 mJ/cm² {input} > {output}
inputs: Live cells (e.g., K562, HepG2; typically 10×10^6 or 20×10^6 cells)
outputs: UV-crosslinked cells with covalently stabilized RBP-RNA complexes

steps.name: eCLIP-seq library preparation — Cells are lysed in iCLIP lysis buffer to release RBP-RNA com
steps.description: Cells are lysed in iCLIP lysis buffer to release RBP-RNA complexes, followed by limited RNA fragmentation with RNase I.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --lysis_buffer iCLIP lysis buffer --RNase RNase I (Ambion) {input} > {output}
outputs: Cell lysate with fragmented RBP-RNA complexes

steps.name: eCLIP-seq library preparation — RBP-RNA complexes are immunoprecipitated using a target-spec
steps.description: RBP-RNA complexes are immunoprecipitated using a target-specific primary antibody and magnetic beads pre-coupled with secondary antibody, followed by stringent washes.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --beads M-280 Sheep Anti-Rabbit IgG Dynabeads (ThermoFisher Scientific 11204D) --RBFOX2_antibody A300-864A lot 002, Bethyl; 10 ug per 20×10^6 cells (Rep1/Rep2) or 5 ug per 10×10^6 cells (RNase I variation) --SLBP_antibody RN045P lot 001, MBL International; 10 ug per 20×10^6 cells --IgG_control_antibody 02-6102 lot 32013, Thermo Fisher Scientific {input} > {output}
inputs: Cell lysate with fragmented RBP-RNA complexes
outputs: Immunoprecipitated RBP-RNA complexes on magnetic beads

parameters.beads: M-280 Sheep Anti-Rabbit IgG Dynabeads (ThermoFisher Scientific 11204D)
parameters.RBFOX2_antibody: A300-864A lot 002, Bethyl; 10 ug per 20×10^6 cells (Rep1/Rep2) or 5 ug per 10×10^6 cells (RNase I variation)
parameters.SLBP_antibody: RN045P lot 001, MBL International; 10 ug per 20×10^6 cells

steps.name: eCLIP-seq library preparation — RNA ends on the bead-bound complexes are dephosphorylated an
steps.description: RNA ends on the bead-bound complexes are dephosphorylated and repaired to prepare for adapter ligation.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --enzymes FastAP (ThermoFisher), T4 PNK (NEB) {input} > {output}
inputs: Immunoprecipitated RBP-RNA complexes on magnetic beads
outputs: Dephosphorylated and end-repaired RNA ends on beads

steps.name: eCLIP-seq library preparation — A barcoded RNA adapter is ligated to the 3′ end of the RNA o
steps.description: A barcoded RNA adapter is ligated to the 3′ end of the RNA on-bead in the presence of high-concentration PEG8000 to achieve >90% ligation efficiency; multiple replicates or RBPs can be uniquely barcoded and pooled at this step.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --enzyme T4 RNA Ligase (NEB) --ligation_format on-bead --additive high concentration PEG8000 --ligation_efficiency >90% {input} > {output}
outputs: 3′ barcoded RNA adapter-ligated RNA on beads (optionally pooled across barcoded replicates)

steps.name: eCLIP-seq library preparation — Samples are resolved on standard SDS-PAGE protein gels and t
steps.description: Samples are resolved on standard SDS-PAGE protein gels and transferred to nitrocellulose membranes; a region ~75 kDa (~150 nt RNA) above the protein molecular weight is excised and treated with proteinase K to recover RNA.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --membrane nitrocellulose --excision_size ~75 kDa above protein size (~150 nt RNA) --proteinase_K NEB {input} > {output}
outputs: Isolated RNA fragments recovered from nitrocellulose membrane

parameters.excision_size: ~75 kDa above protein size (~150 nt RNA)

steps.name: eCLIP-seq library preparation — Isolated RNA is reverse transcribed into cDNA, and excess ol
steps.description: Isolated RNA is reverse transcribed into cDNA, and excess oligonucleotides are removed by ExoSAP-IT treatment.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --reverse_transcriptase AffinityScript (Agilent) --cleanup_enzyme ExoSAP-IT (Affymetrix) {input} > {output}
inputs: RNA fragments isolated from nitrocellulose membrane

steps.name: eCLIP-seq library preparation — A second DNA adapter containing a 5′ random-mer (N5 or N10)
steps.description: A second DNA adapter containing a 5′ random-mer (N5 or N10) is ligated to the 3′ end of the cDNA in the presence of high-concentration PEG8000 and DMSO to improve ligation efficiency and reduce secondary structure inhibition.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --enzyme T4 RNA Ligase (NEB) --random_mer_options N5 or N10 --additives high concentration PEG8000, DMSO {input} > {output}
outputs: cDNA with 3′ DNA adapter ligated (containing 5′ N5 or N10 random-mer UMI)

steps.name: eCLIP-seq library preparation — cDNA is cleaned up using Dynabeads MyOne Silane, and an aliq
steps.description: cDNA is cleaned up using Dynabeads MyOne Silane, and an aliquot is first used for qPCR to determine optimal PCR cycle number before the main sample is PCR amplified.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --cleanup_beads Dynabeads MyOne Silane (ThermoFisher) --qPCR_purpose determine optimal PCR cycle number --PCR_polymerase Q5 (NEB) {input} > {output}

steps.name: eCLIP-seq library preparation — PCR-amplified libraries are size selected by agarose gel ele
steps.description: PCR-amplified libraries are size selected by agarose gel electrophoresis to remove adapter dimers and non-specific products.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --method agarose gel electrophoresis {input} > {output}

steps.name: eCLIP-seq library preparation — Size-selected libraries are sequenced on the Illumina HiSeq
steps.description: Size-selected libraries are sequenced on the Illumina HiSeq 2500 or 4000 platform as paired-end reads; read length depends on the random-mer length used.
steps.command: Illumina HiSeq --sequencing_mode Paired End --read_length_N5 50 bp --read_length_N10 55 bp

steps.name: Normalization of eCLIP signal against SMInput — SMInput samples are processed identically to eCLIP samples t
steps.description: SMInput samples are processed identically to eCLIP samples through the usable read stage to produce comparable read sets for normalization.
steps.command: # unknown_tool {input} > {output}

steps.name: Normalization of eCLIP signal against SMInput — Count the number of eCLIP reads overlapping CLIPper-identifi
steps.description: Count the number of eCLIP reads overlapping CLIPper-identified peaks and the number of SMInput reads overlapping the identical genomic regions, then calculate fold-enrichment normalized by total usable read counts in each dataset.
steps.command: CLIPper ---s hg19 ---o true ----bonferroni true ----superlocal true ----threshold-method binomial ----save-pickle true --read_considered Read 2 only
inputs: Usable eCLIP reads, CLIPper-identified peaks, usable SMInput reads
outputs: Peak-level fold-enrichment values normalized by total usable read counts

parameters.normalization: total usable read counts per dataset

steps.name: Normalization of eCLIP signal against SMInput — Calculate enrichment p-values for each peak using Yates' Chi
steps.description: Calculate enrichment p-values for each peak using Yates' Chi-Square test (Perl) when observed and expected read counts are ≥5, or Fisher Exact Test (R) when observed or expected read counts are below 5.
steps.command: Perl / R statistical computing software --test_primary Yates' Chi-Square test --test_fallback Fisher Exact Test --fallback_threshold observed or expected read count < 5 --min_p_value_chi_square 1e-88 --min_p_value_fisher 2.2e-16 {input} > {output}
inputs: Peak-level read counts for eCLIP and SMInput

steps.name: Normalization of eCLIP signal against SMInput — Perform region-level analysis by counting mapped reads along
steps.description: Perform region-level analysis by counting mapped reads along all transcripts in Gencode v19 (comprehensive) and associating reads with genomic regions by priority order.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --annotation Gencode v19 comprehensive --region_priority coding transcripts (CDS > 5′ UTR / 3′ UTR > intron), then non-coding transcripts (exon > intron) --overlap_requirement majority of read must overlap the region {input} > {output}
inputs: Usable eCLIP reads, Gencode v19 comprehensive transcript annotation
outputs: Region-level read counts per transcript region (CDS, 5′ UTR, 3′ UTR, intron, non-coding exon/intron)

parameters.region_priority: coding transcripts (CDS > 5′ UTR / 3′ UTR > intron), then non-coding transcripts (exon > intron)
parameters.overlap_requirement: majority of read must overlap the region

steps.name: Normalization of eCLIP signal against SMInput — Calculate region-level fold-enrichment, requiring a minimum
steps.description: Calculate region-level fold-enrichment, requiring a minimum of 10 observed or expected reads (expected = eCLIP RPM normalized to SMInput total read depth) per gene for inclusion in the analysis.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --minimum_reads_threshold 10 observed or expected reads --expected_read_calculation eCLIP RPM normalized to SMInput total read depth {input} > {output}
inputs: Region-level read counts for eCLIP and SMInput

parameters.expected_read_calculation: eCLIP RPM normalized to SMInput total read depth

steps.name: Normalization of eCLIP signal against SMInput — Identify motifs with single-nucleotide resolution by locatin
steps.description: Identify motifs with single-nucleotide resolution by locating the 5′ end of each usable Read2 and counting each k-mer within a ±100 nt window around each position, discarding k-mers with unknown sequence (N's), then normalizing against paired SMInput k-mer counts.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --read_end 5′ end of Read2 --flanking_window ±100 nt --kmer_filter discard k-mers containing N's --normalization paired SMInput k-mer counts --iCLIP_normalization_note SMInput from eCLIP Rep1 used for iCLIP normalization (Supplementary Fig. 5a) {input} > {output}
inputs: Usable eCLIP Read2 reads, paired SMInput usable reads
outputs: Normalized k-mer enrichment profiles at single-nucleotide resolution around crosslink sites

parameters.iCLIP_normalization_note: SMInput from eCLIP Rep1 used for iCLIP normalization (Supplementary Fig. 5a)

steps.name: IDR Analysis — Rank SMInput-normalized eCLIP peaks by enrichment p-value fo
steps.description: Rank SMInput-normalized eCLIP peaks by enrichment p-value for both RBFOX2 and SLBP datasets to prepare input for IDR analysis.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --ranking_metric enrichment p-value {input} > {output}
inputs: SMInput-normalized CLIPper peaks for RBFOX2 and SLBP

steps.name: IDR Analysis — Apply the 2012 ENCODE IDR Pipeline to ranked peaks to identi
steps.description: Apply the 2012 ENCODE IDR Pipeline to ranked peaks to identify reproducible peaks across biological replicates.
steps.command: ENCODE IDR Pipeline --pipeline_version 2012 ENCODE IDR Pipeline
inputs: Peaks ranked by enrichment p-value for RBFOX2 and SLBP
outputs: IDR-filtered reproducible peak sets for RBFOX2 and SLBP

steps.name: Public CLIP Database and iCLIP data processing — Raw sequencing data for iCLIP, HITS-CLIP, and PAR-CLIP datas
steps.description: Raw sequencing data for iCLIP, HITS-CLIP, and PAR-CLIP datasets were downloaded from the SRA/ERA as listed in Supplementary Table 1.
steps.command: fastq-dump {accession}

steps.name: Public CLIP Database and iCLIP data processing — Adapter trimming was performed once on the raw reads using c
steps.description: Adapter trimming was performed once on the raw reads using cutadapt.
steps.command: cutadapt --min_length 18

steps.name: Public CLIP Database and iCLIP data processing — Reads were aligned to the genome and datasets with fewer tha
steps.description: Reads were aligned to the genome and datasets with fewer than 100,000 uniquely mapped reads were discarded.
steps.command: STAR --reference RepBase v18.05 (human elements)
outputs: Aligned reads (BAM files); datasets passing read count threshold retained

steps.name: Public CLIP Database and iCLIP data processing — PCR duplicate removal was performed in a library-preparation
steps.description: PCR duplicate removal was performed in a library-preparation-specific manner: for iCLIP datasets, random-mers (if present) were extracted from reads and used for PCR duplicate removal as in eCLIP; for HITS-CLIP and PAR-CLIP datasets, reads with the same mapping start position were assumed to be PCR duplicates and removed.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --iCLIP_dedup_method random-mer based (if present), consistent with eCLIP protocol --HITS-CLIP_dedup_method same start position --PAR-CLIP_dedup_method same start position {input} > {output}

parameters.iCLIP_dedup_method: random-mer based (if present), consistent with eCLIP protocol

steps.name: Public CLIP Database and iCLIP data processing — Peak calling was performed on non-duplicate reads only, foll
steps.description: Peak calling was performed on non-duplicate reads only, following the same approach as eCLIP peak calling (CLIPper).
steps.command: CLIPper ---s hg19 ---o true ----bonferroni true ----superlocal true ----threshold-method binomial ----save-pickle true --read_considered Read 2 only

steps.name: Public CLIP Database and iCLIP data processing — Usable read density plots and smoothed kernel density histog
steps.description: Usable read density plots and smoothed kernel density histograms were generated in Matlab using the distributionPlot package with default settings.
steps.command: Matlab with distributionPlot package --settings default
inputs: Aligned and deduplicated read counts per dataset
outputs: Usable read density plots and kernel density histograms

steps.name: Blocking RBP binding by antisense oligonucleotide — 293T cells are transfected in 24-well format with antisense
steps.description: 293T cells are transfected in 24-well format with antisense oligonucleotides (ASOs) targeting specific RBFOX2 binding sites using RNAiMax as the transfection reagent.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --transfection_reagent RNAiMax --transfection_reagent_volume 1.5 uL --ASO_concentration 100 uM --complex_incubation_volume 50 uL OptiMEM --complex_incubation_time 30 minutes --post_transfection_incubation 36 hours --plate_format 24-well {input} > {output}
inputs: 293T cells, ASOs (100 uM, Isis Pharmaceuticals), RNAiMax (1.5 uL, Thermo Fisher), OptiMEM (50 uL, Thermo Fisher)

steps.name: Blocking RBP binding by antisense oligonucleotide — RNA is isolated from transfected cells using standard TRIzol
steps.description: RNA is isolated from transfected cells using standard TRIzol extraction.
steps.command: TRIzol extraction (standard protocol) --reagent TRIzol --supplier Thermo Fisher

steps.name: Blocking RBP binding by antisense oligonucleotide — Reverse transcription is performed on isolated RNA using Sup
steps.description: Reverse transcription is performed on isolated RNA using SuperScript III to generate cDNA.
steps.command: SuperScript III --supplier Thermo Fisher

steps.name: Blocking RBP binding by antisense oligonucleotide — PCR amplification of splicing products is performed using Ph
steps.description: PCR amplification of splicing products is performed using Phusion DNA polymerase with primers located in flanking constitutive exons to detect exon inclusion and exclusion isoforms.
steps.command: Phusion DNA polymerase --supplier Thermo Fisher --primer_location flanking constitutive exons
inputs: cDNA, primers in flanking constitutive exons
outputs: PCR amplicons (exon inclusion and exclusion products)

steps.name: Blocking RBP binding by antisense oligonucleotide — PCR products are resolved by agarose gel electrophoresis and
steps.description: PCR products are resolved by agarose gel electrophoresis and imaged to visualize inclusion and exclusion bands.
steps.command: # unknown_tool {input} > {output}
inputs: PCR amplicons (exon inclusion and exclusion products)

steps.name: Blocking RBP binding by antisense oligonucleotide — Band intensities from gel images are quantified using ImageJ
steps.description: Band intensities from gel images are quantified using ImageJ to calculate percent spliced in (PSI/exon inclusion percentage) for each condition; statistical analysis is performed using Student's t-test.
steps.command: ImageJ --statistical_test Student's t-test --error_bars sample standard deviation

steps.name: RBFOX2-knockdown transcriptome profiling by microarray — 293T cells were transduced with 3 independent lentiviral shR
steps.description: 293T cells were transduced with 3 independent lentiviral shRNAs targeting RBFOX2 (TRCN0000074544, TRCN0000074546, TRCN0000074543) and a non-targeting control (SHC016), with 3 independent transductions per shRNA, followed by 10 days of puromycin selection.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --shRNA_targeting TRCN0000074544, TRCN0000074546, TRCN0000074543 (Sigma) --non_targeting_control SHC016 (Sigma) --replicates_per_shRNA 3 independent transductions --selection_agent Puromycin --selection_duration 10 days {input} > {output}
outputs: Stably transduced, puromycin-selected 293T cell pools with RBFOX2 knockdown or non-targeting control

steps.name: RBFOX2-knockdown transcriptome profiling by microarray — RBFOX2 protein knockdown was validated by western blotting u
steps.description: RBFOX2 protein knockdown was validated by western blotting using RBFOX2 and GAPDH antibodies, imaged on the Odyssey fluorescent imager, and quantitated with median local background correction.
steps.command: Image Studio Lite --RBFOX2_antibody A300-864A (Bethyl) --GAPDH_antibody ab8245 (Abcam) --imager Odyssey fluorescent imager (LiCor) --background_correction median local background correction

steps.name: RBFOX2-knockdown transcriptome profiling by microarray — Total RNA was isolated from transduced cells using Trizol ex
steps.description: Total RNA was isolated from transduced cells using Trizol extraction and microarray libraries were prepared using the WT Expression Kit.
steps.command: WT Expression Kit (Ambion) --RNA_isolation_reagent Trizol (Thermo Fisher Scientific) --library_prep_kit WT Expression Kit (Ambion)
outputs: Amplified and labeled RNA samples ready for microarray hybridization

steps.name: RBFOX2-knockdown transcriptome profiling by microarray — Labeled RNA samples were hybridized to Affymetrix HTA2.0 mic
steps.description: Labeled RNA samples were hybridized to Affymetrix HTA2.0 microarrays and scanned.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --array_platform Affymetrix HTA2.0 {input} > {output}

steps.name: RBFOX2-knockdown transcriptome profiling by microarray — All probe signals were RMA-normalized using Affymetrix Expre
steps.description: All probe signals were RMA-normalized using Affymetrix Expression Console.
steps.command: Affymetrix Expression Console --normalization_method RMA

steps.name: RBFOX2-knockdown transcriptome profiling by microarray — Probes corresponding to cassette exons (exclusion junction,
steps.description: Probes corresponding to cassette exons (exclusion junction, upstream and downstream inclusion junction, and inclusion exonic probes) were identified and normalized against the average per-gene signal to remove gene expression changes.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --probe_types exclusion junction, upstream inclusion junction, downstream inclusion junction, inclusion exonic probes --normalization average signal on a per-gene basis {input} > {output}
outputs: Per-gene-normalized residuals for cassette exon probes

parameters.probe_types: exclusion junction, upstream inclusion junction, downstream inclusion junction, inclusion exonic probes
parameters.normalization: average signal on a per-gene basis

steps.name: RBFOX2-knockdown transcriptome profiling by microarray — Student's t-test was performed on residuals for inclusion an
steps.description: Student's t-test was performed on residuals for inclusion and exclusion probes separately to identify robust splicing changes, using thresholds on p-value and SepScore (normalized change in exclusion minus normalized change in inclusion).
steps.command: custom code --statistical_test Student's t-test --p_value_threshold ≤ 0.001 --SepScore_threshold |SepScore| ≥ 0.5 --SepScore_definition normalized change in exclusion minus normalized change in inclusion --exons_TRCN0000074544 197 --exons_TRCN0000074543 217
inputs: Per-gene-normalized residuals for cassette exon probes
outputs: Sets of differentially spliced cassette exons per shRNA (197 exons for TRCN0000074544; 217 exons for TRCN0000074543)

parameters.SepScore_definition: normalized change in exclusion minus normalized change in inclusion

steps.name: RBFOX2-knockdown transcriptome profiling by microarray — Differentially spliced cassette exons were overlapped with R
steps.description: Differentially spliced cassette exons were overlapped with RBFOX2 eCLIP peaks by associating each eCLIP peak with a cassette exon if the peak was located anywhere in the flanking upstream or downstream intron.
steps.command: # unknown_tool --shRNA_used_for_eCLIP_overlap TRCN0000074544 (197 events) --peak_association_rule eCLIP peak located at any position in flanking upstream or downstream intron {input} > {output}
inputs: Differentially spliced cassette exons (197 events from TRCN0000074544) and RBFOX2 eCLIP peak calls
outputs: Cassette exons with associated nearby RBFOX2 eCLIP binding peaks

parameters.peak_association_rule: eCLIP peak located at any position in flanking upstream or downstream intron

steps.name: eCLIP-seq data processing — Standard HiSeq demultiplexing followed by inline barcode dem
steps.description: Standard HiSeq demultiplexing followed by inline barcode demultiplexing of eCLIP libraries using custom scripts; the random-mer (UMI) is appended to the read name for later use.
steps.software: Custom scripts (Illumina HiSeq demultiplexing + in-house inline barcode demultiplexing)
steps.command: hiSeqDemultiplex.py {raw_fastq}
inputs: Raw FASTQ reads from HiSeq sequencing
outputs: Demultiplexed FASTQ reads with random-mer appended to read names

steps.name: eCLIP-seq data processing — Adapter trimming of demultiplexed reads; reads shorter than
steps.description: Adapter trimming of demultiplexed reads; reads shorter than 18 bp after trimming are discarded.
steps.command: cutadapt --min_length 18

steps.name: eCLIP-seq data processing — Reads are first mapped against human repetitive elements in
steps.description: Reads are first mapped against human repetitive elements in RepBase to segregate repeat-mapping reads for separate analysis.
steps.command: STAR --reference RepBase v18.05 (human elements)
outputs: Repeat-mapped reads (segregated) and unmapped reads passed to genomic alignment

steps.name: eCLIP-seq data processing — Non-repeat reads are mapped against the full human genome (h
steps.description: Non-repeat reads are mapped against the full human genome (hg19) including a splice junction database.
steps.command: STAR --reference RepBase v18.05 (human elements)
outputs: Aligned BAM files (uniquely mapping reads against hg19)

steps.name: eCLIP-seq data processing — PCR duplicate removal using a custom script that removes dup
steps.description: PCR duplicate removal using a custom script that removes duplicates sharing identical Read1 start position, Read2 start position, and random-mer sequence, yielding 'Usable' reads.
steps.command: Custom PCR duplicate removal script --deduplication_criteria identical Read1 start position, Read2 start position, and random-mer sequence

parameters.deduplication_criteria: identical Read1 start position, Read2 start position, and random-mer sequence

steps.name: eCLIP-seq data processing — For eCLIP datasets with multiple inline barcodes, usable rea
steps.description: For eCLIP datasets with multiple inline barcodes, usable reads from each barcode are merged into a single dataset prior to peak calling.
steps.command: custom_scripts merge_barcodes {per_barcode_deduplicated_bam_files} > {merged_usable_reads_bam}

steps.name: eCLIP-seq data processing — Cluster/peak identification is performed on usable reads usi
steps.description: Cluster/peak identification is performed on usable reads using CLIPper, considering Read 2 only (enriched for termination at the crosslink site).
steps.command: CLIPper ---s hg19 ---o true ----bonferroni true ----superlocal true ----threshold-method binomial ----save-pickle true --read_considered Read 2 only

steps.name: eCLIP-seq data processing — All genome browser tracks are RPM-normalized against the tot
steps.description: All genome browser tracks are RPM-normalized against the total number of usable reads in each dataset for visualization on the UCSC Genome Browser.
steps.command: Custom normalization scripts --normalization RPM (reads per million usable reads)

steps.name: eCLIP-seq data processing — Downsampling analysis performed by 100 iterations of randoml
steps.description: Downsampling analysis performed by 100 iterations of randomly permuting uniquely mapped reads, selecting the top N reads, and applying PCR duplicate removal to identify usable reads at each depth.
steps.command: Custom downsampling scripts --iterations 100 --selection top N reads per iteration --note For iCLIP with multiple cDNA-size libraries, only library with highest percent usable was used
outputs: Usable read counts at various downsampled depths (100 iterations each)

parameters.note: For iCLIP with multiple cDNA-size libraries, only library with highest percent usable was used

steps.name: eCLIP-seq data processing — De novo motif finding for RBFOX2 iCLIP and eCLIP clusters us
steps.description: De novo motif finding for RBFOX2 iCLIP and eCLIP clusters using HOMER's findMotifs program, comparing cluster sequences against background regions matched by genic region type.
steps.command: HOMER findMotifs ---p 4 ---rna true ---S 10 ---len 5,6,7,8,9 --background 3 random same-sized regions per cluster matched by genic region type (introns, 3′UTRs, etc.)
inputs: CLIPper cluster sequences (eCLIP and iCLIP for RBFOX2)

parameters.background: 3 random same-sized regions per cluster matched by genic region type (introns, 3′UTRs, etc.)

steps.name: eCLIP-seq data processing — Cluster location pie charts are generated by counting the to
steps.description: Cluster location pie charts are generated by counting the total number of bases covered by peaks for each genic region type.
steps.command: custom_annotation_script --input {clipper_peaks} --output {pie_chart}
inputs: CLIPper peak/cluster calls with genic region annotations

steps.name: eCLIP-seq data processing — For RBFOX2 RNase I shearing analysis, each cluster is annota
steps.description: For RBFOX2 RNase I shearing analysis, each cluster is annotated by overlapping genic region type and peaks per region are counted.
steps.command: custom_annotation_script --input {clipper_peaks} --output {pie_chart}
inputs: CLIPper clusters from RBFOX2 RNase I shearing eCLIP
outputs: Peak counts per genic region type

parameters.annotation_priority: same region priority order as region-level analysis

steps.name: eCLIP-seq data processing — For motif enrichment analysis before and after SMInput norma
steps.description: For motif enrichment analysis before and after SMInput normalization (RBFOX2, PUM2, TARDBP, TRA2A), CLIPper-identified clusters are extended to a minimum of 100 nt centered around the cluster midpoint.
steps.command: custom_scripts merge_barcodes {per_barcode_deduplicated_bam_files} > {merged_usable_reads_bam}
inputs: CLIPper cluster coordinates for RBFOX2, PUM2, TARDBP, and TRA2A
outputs: Extended cluster regions (≥100 nt) for motif enrichment analysis

parameters.extension: minimum 100 nt centered on CLIPper cluster midpoint

snakefile_content:
"""
Snakemake workflow: 10_1038_nmeth_3810

Reproducible pipeline generated from paper (10.1038/nmeth.3810). Assays: eCLIP-seq library preparation, Normalization of eCLIP signal against SMInput, IDR Analysis, Public CLIP Database and iCLIP data processing, Blocking RBP binding by antisense oligonucleotide, RBFOX2-knockdown transcriptome profiling by microarray, eCLIP-seq data processing.

Generated by researcher-ai PipelineBuilder.
"""

from snakemake.utils import min_version
min_version("7.0")

configfile: "config.yaml"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Samples
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Populate this list from your samplesheet or config.
SAMPLES = config.get('samples', [])

# Datasets referenced by this pipeline:
DATASETS = ['GSE77634', 'GSE77339', 'GSE77629', 'GSE77633', 'GSE80039']

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# rule all: declare final targets
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
rule all:
    input:
        "Normalized k-mer enrichment profiles at single-nucleotide resolution around crosslink sites",
        "IDR-filtered reproducible peak sets for RBFOX2 and SLBP",
        "Usable read density plots and kernel density histograms",
        "PSI values per condition, p-values",
        "Cassette exons with associated nearby RBFOX2 eCLIP binding peaks",
        "Extended cluster regions (≥100 nt) for motif enrichment analysis",


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# eCLIP-seq library preparation — RNA-binding protein–RNA interactions are stabilized by UV cr
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
rule eclip_seq_library_preparation_step1:
    input:
        "Live cells (e.g., K562, HepG2; typically 10×10^6 or 20×10^6 cells)",
    output:
        "UV-crosslinked cells with covalently stabilized RBP-RNA complexes"
    params:
        UV_wavelength=config.get("UV_wavelength", "254 nm"),
        UV_dose=config.get("UV_dose", "400 mJ/cm²"),
    threads: 4
    resources:
        mem_mb=8192,
        runtime=120,
    log: "logs/eclip_seq_library_preparation_step1.log"
    shell:
        "# unknown_tool --UV_wavelength 254 nm --UV_dose 400 mJ/cm² {input} > {output} 2> {log}"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# eCLIP-seq library preparation — Cells are lysed in iCLIP lysis buffer to release RBP-RNA com
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
rule eclip_seq_library_preparation_step2:
    input:
        "UV-crosslinked cells",
        rules.eclip_seq_library_preparation_step1.output,
    output:
        "Cell lysate with fragmented RBP-RNA complexes"
    params:
        lysis_buffer=config.get("lysis_buffer", "iCLIP lysis buffer"),
        RNase=config.get("RNase", "RNase I (Ambion)"),
    threads: 4
    resources:
        mem_mb=8192,
        runtime=120,
    log: "logs/eclip_seq_library_preparation_step2.log"
    shell:
        "# unknown_tool --lysis_buffer iCLIP lysis buffer --RNase RNase I (Ambion) {input} > {output} 2> {log}"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# eCLIP-seq library preparation — RBP-RNA complexes are immunoprecipitated using a target-spec
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
rule eclip_seq_library_preparation_step3:
    input:
        "Cell lysate with fragmented RBP-RNA complexes",
        rules.eclip_seq_library_preparation_step2.output,
    output:
        "Immunoprecipitated RBP-RNA complexes on magnetic beads"
    params:
